summary.oneLine: A rich melon-seed thickened soup with pepper-tomato base and greens; served with rice, yam, or swallow.
notes: Egusi varies widely by household: proteins, greens (spinach/ugwu/bitterleaf), and seasoning are flexible.
This SSA baseline emphasizes a cooked-down pepper-tomato base, then egusi thickening, then greens.
Egusi is calorie-dense due to seeds/oil; SSA nutrition pipeline should compute final values from ingredient quantities.
Egusi (melon seeds) may be considered a seed allergen for some households.
Optional dried fish/crayfish/shrimp are common in some variants—apply household allergen preferences.

ingredients.label: Ground egusi (melon seeds)
notes: Use finely ground egusi; if coarse, blend briefly.
If your household treats egusi as non-optional, remove the '_optional' in your ingredient catalog or alias rules.

ingredients.label: Red palm oil
notes: For lighter versions, use half red palm oil + half neutral oil.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil
notes: Adjust total oil based on desired richness and egusi fat release.

ingredients.label: Yellow onion
notes: One for base; one optional for broth/protein pot.

ingredients.label: Tomatoes
notes: Some egusi versions are less tomato-heavy; keep optional if you prefer.

ingredients.label: Scotch bonnet (or habanero)
notes: Use half for medium heat; remove seeds for milder heat.

ingredients.label: Beef or chicken stock
notes: Or use water plus seasoning; adjust salt carefully.

ingredients.label: Water
notes: Total liquid varies with protein choice and greens moisture.

ingredients.label: Salt
notes: Start lower if using salted stock/cubes; adjust at end.

ingredients.label: Spinach (or other leafy greens)
notes: Chopped. Alternative greens: bitterleaf (optional), pumpkin leaves, kale.

ingredients.label: Bitterleaf (washed)
notes: Adds classic bitterness; rinse/washed as appropriate.

ingredients.label: Smoked dried fish
notes: Optional; remove bones and apply fish allergen rules.

ingredients.label: Ground dried crayfish
notes: Optional; shellfish allergen.

ingredients.label: Dried shrimp
notes: Optional; shellfish allergen.

ingredients.label: Beef (stew cuts)
notes: Choose one protein set below; mix-and-match is common.

steps.title: Prep protein and start broth
steps.body: Choose your protein (beef, goat, or chicken). Season lightly with salt and black pepper. Add to a pot with 1 chopped onion (optional), thyme/bay leaf (optional), stock cube (optional), and enough water/stock to cover. Simmer until protein is tender (beef/goat 35–55 min; chicken 20–30 min). Reserve the broth.

steps.title: Make pepper-tomato base (optional but recommended)
steps.body: Blend (or finely chop) tomatoes (optional), bell pepper (optional), scotch bonnet (optional), garlic, ginger, and part of the onion. Set aside.

hazards.message: Use gloves when handling very hot peppers; avoid touching eyes/face and ventilate when blending.

steps.title: Sauté and cook down the base
steps.body: Heat oils (red palm oil optional + neutral oil) over medium heat. Add remaining chopped onion and sauté 3–5 minutes. Add blended base (if using) and simmer uncovered 10–15 minutes until thickened and the raw taste cooks off.

hazards.message: Pepper-tomato base may splatter; reduce heat and stir carefully.

steps.title: Bloom and thicken with egusi
steps.body: Reduce heat to medium-low. Sprinkle in ground egusi while stirring to prevent clumps. Stir 2–3 minutes so egusi absorbs oil and begins to ‘bloom.’ Add 1–2 cups reserved broth (or stock/water) gradually, stirring until a thick paste forms, then loosen to a stew consistency.

steps.title: Simmer with protein and optional umami builders
steps.body: Add cooked protein and 2–3 cups broth (as needed). Add smoked fish/crayfish/dried shrimp (optional) if allowed by household allergens. Simmer gently 15–20 minutes so flavors meld and egusi fully cooks.

steps.title: Add greens, finish, and rest
steps.body: Stir in spinach and bitterleaf (optional). Simmer 3–5 minutes until wilted. Taste and adjust salt/pepper. Turn off heat and rest 8–10 minutes before serving to allow body to set.

suggestions: Serve with rice, yam, plantain, or swallow (fufu/eba) depending on household preference.
A small side of pepper sauce can be served for extra heat if desired.

reheatNotes: Reheat gently with a splash of water/stock; egusi thickens as it cools.
Avoid hard boiling after greens are added to preserve color and texture.

substitutions.notes: Red palm oil adds color and distinctive flavor; neutral oil yields a lighter profile.
Habanero is closest in fruity heat; start small and adjust.
Use hearty greens if needed; simmer a bit longer for tenderness.

equipmentFallbacks.doInstead: Finely chop tomatoes/peppers/onion/garlic/ginger; simmer longer to break down. A rustic texture is acceptable.

allergenConsiderations.notes: Use stock and careful seasoning for umami instead of seafood-based boosters.
Crayfish and dried shrimp are shellfish triggers; omit entirely if active.

knownGood: Egusi should be fully cooked (no raw seed taste) and thicken the broth to coat a spoon.
Base (if used) should be cooked down to avoid a raw pepper-tomato flavor.
Greens should be wilted but not cooked to mush.

commonIssues.likelyCause: Added too quickly or without enough stirring/oil contact
commonIssues.fix: Sprinkle gradually while stirring; bloom in oil first, then add broth slowly.

commonIssues.likelyCause: Base not cooked down or bitterleaf not washed enough
commonIssues.fix: Reduce base longer; rinse bitterleaf thoroughly and add later in cooking.

commonIssues.likelyCause: Excess oil relative to egusi and liquid
commonIssues.fix: Reduce oil next time; skim excess oil or add more broth and simmer gently to emulsify.
